artifacts.content:
From: caleb.holst@gmail.com
To: erin.holst@protonmail.com
Subject: Tonight, therapy, and game stuff

Hey love,

Kids are finally down (Jonah only got up twice for water, which feels like a win), so I wanted to write this out while it’s fresh.

Dr. Laird keeps circling back to New Year’s Eve and how that fight about me closing late at Mountain Gate and missing daycare pickup wasn’t just about one night. She actually asked today if there’s anywhere in my life where I *do* consistently put family first, and the only concrete example I could think of was that Sunday 8–9:30 p.m. “budget duty” block I carved out during the Opportunity Bank repayment plan.

Remember July 2022 through August 2023, when we were sending them $275 every month so we didn’t lose the apartment over that mess of rent and medical bills? Every Sunday at the kitchen table at 1842 Frontier, I’d go line by line through statements, call the Opportunity Bank collections rep, and tweak the spreadsheet. Fourteen months of that grind, just so we wouldn’t end up in Helena Municipal Court or back at my parents’ place.

I told her I keep building GURPS characters with Sense of Duty (Party) and insane Will because that version of me—the guy who sat there every Sunday and didn’t skip a week—feels more real than the dude who forgets to text you when Kyle dumps a last‑minute draft on me.

Anyway, I’m going to hard‑bake Sense of Duty (Family) into the frontier‑city cleric. No more PCs who treat their spouses like background NPCs. If you’re up for it Sunday, maybe we can use our no‑screens hour to look at the calendar and block off “Opportunity Bank‑style” family time again.

Love you,
Caleb

From: caleb.holst@gmail.com
To: lucas.holst88@yahoo.com
Subject: Why this half-orc thing is hitting so hard

Hey Luke,

Been meaning to write since you asked about the new frontier campaign.

I’m leaning into that Half‑Orc (+10) template I mentioned, basically to process some of the crap around what happened to you outside Mountain Gate on March 12, 2023. I keep thinking about you getting jumped on the sidewalk by the shop, called a “half‑breed,” and me standing there afterward trying to explain to the cops why you were bleeding in front of the store I’m supposed to keep “family‑friendly.” It’s been in my therapy homework and campaign notes since last summer.

At the same time, I can’t stop tying it to all the other borderline‑status stuff: the welfare check at 1842 Frontier last February when the sheriff and CPS worker, Danielle Kramer, came by after Mara’s meltdown and the downstairs neighbor called in “yelling.” That brief little note in Helena city records was enough to make us feel like the problem apartment in the building. Status -1 in real life.

And then there’s your own court junk—those deferred‑sentence check‑ins at the Cascade County Courthouse every couple months since the I‑15 stop outside Great Falls on July 9, 2022. I’ve driven you up there enough now that it’s just part of the calendar, like “Lucas Duty (Great Falls run).”

So yeah, the PC’s got Social Stigma (Minority Group) and Status -1, and a whole tangle of family obligations. It’s all just our stuff with fantasy serial numbers filed off. If that ever feels too close for you, tell me and I’ll dial it back.

Love you,
C

metadata.subject: Why this half-orc thing is hitting so hard

artifacts.content:
Google Keep note
Title: Campaign + real life baggage dump

Jotting this down before I lose it:

1. Guard captain NPC is 100% Colin with the serial numbers filed off. The whole Rank 2 “I hold your livelihood in my hands” vibe is straight from the May 2023 six‑month PIP at Mountain Gate—three cash‑drawer discrepancies totalling $487 plus that Friday Night Magic complaint where I snapped at the kid. Colin’s “we need to rebuild trust” speech goes right into the temple hierarchy.

2. The cleric’s crisis of faith and Religious Rank 0–1 arc is Erin’s 2021 crash in disguise: August 2021–March 2022, twice‑weekly sessions with Dr. Kendra Lowell at St. Peter’s Behavioral Health, parents wiring $600/month so we could keep up with the $1,050 rent at 1842 Frontier, and the bathroom on October 14, 2021 that I still can’t think about without shaking. That’s the “left for dead, crawled back” piece.

3. Vow of Poverty = Mara’s June 2022 ER bill ($2,870 after insurance) on the Chase Freedom, 18‑month plan that didn’t clear until December 2023. That constant “do we buy groceries or make the minimum?” feeling.

4. Secret cult arc is just New Dawn Fellowship in the Graceview basement from 2003–2007: Dad and Mom dropping ~$18k, sheriff’s office poking around in ’06, Renee finally digging out by Spring 2019.

Remember: none of this gets explained at the table. Players just see “stressed priest with debts and a shady past,” not Erin, Mom, or Colin.

From: caleb.holst@gmail.com
To: mom.renee.holst@hotmail.com
Subject: Quick health + kids update

Hey Mom,

Figured I’d send an actual email instead of a string of half‑coherent texts.

Physically I’m… okay-ish. Still on the CPAP since the sleep apnea diagnosis at St. Peter’s back in March 2023. Dr. Lang says crashing after 3–4 hours on my feet at Mountain Gate is “expected,” but it’s frustrating when I gas out after 20–30 minutes chasing Mara and Jonah around Memorial Park. The Helena Family Clinic doc in April said my blood pressure is borderline, so I’ve cut way back on energy drinks and the 1–2 a.m. layout marathons.

Mentally, the panic stuff is still there but better. I’m doing weekly telehealth CBT with Dr. Melissa Kearns through St. Peter’s Behavioral Health—Wednesdays at 6:30, so Erin covers bedtime. That all started after the bad episode during the April 15, 2023 Pathfinder prerelease at the shop, when the crowd noise tipped me over. Between that and the earlier Call of Cthulhu panic on May 12, 2023, horror games are on a short leash for now.

Mara’s doing okay—still some articulation work from the St. Peter’s speech therapist’s May 2022 plan, but she loves first grade at Bryant. Jonah’s anemia check with Dr. Patel at Helena Pediatrics in January made me more paranoid; I keep thinking of him as having “-2 HP” compared to other kids, which is a very Caleb way to worry.

We’re still tight on money—IRS audit from April over the $3,200 DriveThruRPG income means a $900 payment plan—but we’re managing. Sunday coffee at your place this week? I can bring muffins and the kids’ new dice.

Love,
Caleb

Title: Court + money days (don’t overbook)

All‑day event series in Google Calendar

Notes:

— March 4, 2026 – 9:00 a.m.–1:00 p.m.
Event: Chapter 13 confirmation hearing
Location: U.S. Bankruptcy Court, Butte
Details: Drive down from Helena early. Bring folder with St. Vincent NICU bills from Mara’s November–December 2018 stay in Billings, plus credit card statements (about $18,000 medical + $9,000 cards). Erin coming if Kara can take the kids.

— Every 2nd Friday (odd months) – 8:00 a.m.–4:00 p.m.
Event: Drive Lucas to deferred‑sentence check‑in
Location: Cascade County Courthouse, Great Falls
Details: Started August 2022 after his I‑15 possession stop on July 9, 2022. Pack snacks, tablet for kids if they come. Treat as “Duty (Family)” day—no game nights.

— Sundays – 8:00–9:30 p.m. (July 2022–August 2023, archived)
Event: Opportunity Bank repayment grind
Location: Kitchen table, 1842 Frontier Ave
Details: 14‑month, $275/month plan after falling behind on rent/medical. Go line‑by‑line through statements, call collections rep if anything looks off. This is the template for the cleric’s high Will / Sense of Duty in the frontier campaign.

— Reminder: Don’t schedule in‑store marathons (14‑hour GURPS days like 7/9/21) near these; last time ended with me fainting at Mountain Gate and urgent care follow‑up.

From: caleb.holst@gmail.com
To: dad.derrin.holst@outlook.com
Subject: Money, Lucas, and the new game thing

Hey Dad,

I’ve been thinking a lot about that family meeting at your place on November 6, 2022—Lucas tearing into me about the $3,000 I borrowed in August 2021 and the fact I’d only managed to get $500 back to him, you telling us we needed to “stop living like church mice.” None of that was fun, but it’s been rattling around in my head while I sketch this new frontier‑city campaign.

On top of that we’ve got the Chapter 13 stuff hanging over March 4, 2026 in Butte (the $18k from Mara’s NICU at St. Vincent in Billings in late 2018 plus the $9k in card debt), and the old history of you nearly losing the Helena house in the 2008–2010 mess and then the $42k Chapter 13 you filed in May 2019 at the Lewis and Clark County Courthouse. It’s like we’ve had “Struggling wealth” on our sheet for three generations.

I’m trying to turn some of that into something useful in the game. The Local Cultural (Borderlander) template I wrote basically bakes in generational debt—the PC’s dad loses his smithy in a court dispute that’s just our 2019 bankruptcy with the names filed off. I’m also capping Wealth at Struggling/Comfortable for my players; no Multimillionaires swooping in to erase problems.

I don’t know if that makes any of this easier, but it at least feels like I’m telling the truth about how money and obligation actually work for us.

Love,
Caleb

metadata.subject: Money, Lucas, and the new game thing

artifacts.content:
Apple Notes
Title: Health + house rules brain dump

Just to have this all in one place:

ST: Keeping my own PCs around ST 11–13. After that hypertensive episode lifting boxes at Mountain Gate on March 4, 2023—ending up with Dr. Lauren McIntyre at St. Peter’s, 50 mg metoprolol, and the pending echo—it feels gross to power‑fantasy a ST 16 bruiser. Add in Lucas wrecking his shoulder trying to deadlift 405 at Capital City Health & Fitness in August 2022 and my 11/17/21 back strain hauling 18 Warhammer/D&D boxes (plus the Montana State Fund adjuster hinting it was “my fault” for overexertion), and I’m just done glorifying meathead strength.

DX: Cap 15 hard. Stroke at St. Peter’s in June 2022, 45–60 minutes of hand‑eye drills three times a week for six months with Dr. Ellison at the neuro clinic, plus Mara’s CP diagnosis at Shodair in September 2021 and those 45‑minute PT blocks with Jenna Collins twice a week—no more parkour gods who never miss a roll.

HP/HT: Cinematic buffer stays. Between my own rear‑end collision on I‑15 (3/3/22, concussion + cracked ribs with Dr. Carrow), the kidney stone ER bill ($3,800 on 5/17/20), COVID round two in November 2021 (18 days off work, Erin on doubles at St. Peter’s), and Jonah’s febrile seizure on 5/21/24, I don’t need hyper‑lethal bleed‑outs.

Will/Per/FP: Keep Will and Per decoupled from IQ. Model burnout on Holiday 2023 at the shop—10–12 hour shifts, 6 days/week, plus freelance until 1–2 a.m., nearly fainting on 12/22 when Tom sent me home. FP should snowball over days, not just scenes.
